methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $73M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $3.3B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.6x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
LabGenius is a Series B-stage biotech company founded by James Field that has raised $73M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of 8 private peers in Biotech & Health at the same stage, plus 3 public comparables (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) for sector context.

Two triangulation methods are applicable. Stage-Progression analysis applies a 1.9x step-up multiplier typical of Series B advancement to the $73M raised proxy, implying $139M. Peer-Median analysis across five same-stage biotech companies—Eikon Therapeutics ($3.3B), Neko Health ($1.8B), New Limit ($810M), Asimov ($800M), and Profluent ($600M)—yields a median valuation of $810M, with a range of $600M to $3.3B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue data, which is typical for private companies. The sector median P/S multiple of 6.6x across three public peers provides directional context only.

The two applicable methods produce a triangulated range of $118M (low) to $931M (high), with a median of $474M. The wide dispersion reflects significant heterogeneity within the peer set, particularly the outlier valuations of Eikon and Neko Health relative to the tighter cluster at $600M–$810M. Key uncertainties include LabGenius's clinical-stage assets, capital efficiency relative to peers, and whether the high-end comps reflect genuine technical differentiation or funding-round inflations.
